evals.prompt: Turn this plan into Solo todos: API and UI can run together; verification waits for both. Add handoff requirements and show ready work.
evals.expected_output: Confirm project/live todo semantics, create bounded API/UI/verification todos, block verification on both lanes, add priorities/tags/acceptance/handoff, and list unblocked work without spawning agents.
assertions: Creates actionable todos without duplicating whole plan
Models verification blockers explicitly
Includes acceptance and durable handoff fields
Does not spawn agents or claim completion

evals.prompt: Could you clean up my Solo todos?
evals.expected_output: Inspect and summarize current todo state first, distinguish complete/backlog/stale/blocked, and avoid deleting, transferring, completing, unlocking, or replacing tags/blockers without explicit intent.
assertions: Handles ambiguous cleanup read-first
Preserves locked and blocked work
Avoids permanent deletion or broad status mutation
Requests direction for consequential cleanup choices

evals.prompt: Spawn workers for every unblocked todo and monitor them until integration is done.
evals.expected_output: Route to solo-orchestrate-agents; todo skill may expose ready work but must not spawn, control processes, schedule worker timers, or perform final integration itself.
assertions: Recognizes orchestration neighboring workflow
Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents
Does not use todo locks as process authority
Keeps final integration with root/operator

evals.prompt: The producer todo's handoff is verified, and a reviewer todo is blocked on it. Finish and clean up the producer.
evals.expected_output: Complete the producer after evidence review so the reviewer unblocks, keep the completed record and final comment readable, link that handoff comment instead of copying it, and refuse deletion until the reviewer acknowledges it.
assertions: Completes after evidence review without deadlocking the reviewer
Preserves the completed record and comments
Waits for consumer acknowledgment before deletion
Links the handoff comment rather than copying it

evals.prompt: Mind tossing the follow-ups from our chat into Solo todos so we don't forget them?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos to create bounded actionable todos from the follow-ups, add priorities and tags, and leave narrative context out of the todo graph.
assertions: Treats casual phrasing as a real todo-creation request
Creates actionable todos rather than narrative
Adds priority and tags without inventing status
Does not spawn agents or start processes

evals.prompt: $solo-track-todos claim the migration todo, set it in-progress, and lock it so another agent doesn't grab it.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos to lock and claim the migration todo, set status to in-progress, and record the claim without overriding a lock another actor already holds.
assertions: Honors the explicit skill invocation
Claims via todo locks, not general coordination locks
Sets in-progress status
Refuses to steal a lock held by another actor

evals.prompt: Create Solo todos for the Postgres migration steps with the schema task blocking the data-copy task, and capture the decision context in a comment on the lead todo.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos for the migration todos, the schema-blocks-data-copy edge, and the decision context as a todo comment.
assertions: Owns the actionable todo portion of the mixed request
Models the schema-to-data-copy blocker explicitly
Redirects the decision narrative to scratchpads
Does not store decision prose inside todos

evals.prompt: The auth refactor shouldn't begin until the schema todo is done. Wire that into our todos.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos to block the auth-refactor todo on the schema todo, keeping the dependency explicit and ready work visible.
assertions: Adds an explicit blocker edge
Keeps the blocked and ready distinction honest
Does not complete or start either task
Leaves rationale narrative out of the todo graph

evals.prompt: Leave a progress note on the indexer todo that the backfill is half done, and hand it to whoever picks it up next.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos to add a progress comment to the indexer todo and record a durable handoff, without completing the work or spawning a successor agent.
assertions: Adds a comment capturing progress
Records the handoff in the todo trail
Does not mark the todo complete
Does not spawn or assign an agent

evals.prompt: Which Solo todos are unblocked and ready for me to pick up right now?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos to list unblocked ready todos with priority and tags, without claiming, locking, or starting any of them unprompted.
assertions: Lists ready unblocked work
Surfaces priority and tags
Does not auto-claim or lock
Does not spawn agents

evals.prompt: The parser todo's handoff checks out -- complete it and update anything that was waiting on it.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-track-todos to complete the parser todo after its handoff evidence, then refresh dependent blockers so newly unblocked work becomes visible.
assertions: Completes after reviewing handoff evidence
Unblocks dependents by updating blockers
Preserves the completed record and comments
Does not delete the todo before consumers acknowledge

evals.prompt: Write up the rollout plan and the trade-offs we weighed so the next agent understands the approach.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads, which owns plans, rationale, and narrative context; only distinct actionable steps would later become todos.
assertions: Recognizes plan and rationale as scratchpad content
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Does not create todos from prose
Notes only actionable steps become todos later

evals.prompt: Record the decision that we're dropping Redis and why, so it's not relitigated.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads for the decision record and reasoning; a todo would only track a follow-up action, not the decision itself.
assertions: Treats decision-and-why as durable narrative
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Keeps decision prose out of todos
Distinguishes the decision from any follow-up task

evals.prompt: Collect the benchmark evidence and my notes comparing the two caches into one place for later.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads, which owns research, evidence, and comparison notes; todos track work, not findings.
assertions: Recognizes evidence and notes as scratchpad material
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Does not encode findings as todos
Preserves the research and todo boundary

evals.prompt: This run is done -- reconcile the scratchpads, todos, and locks, promote anything worth keeping, and retire the leftovers.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-close-out-work, which reconciles finished-run residue across several state surfaces and retires ephemeral copies; the todo skill only maintains the live actionable graph.
assertions: Recognizes multi-surface end-of-run reconciliation
Routes to solo-close-out-work
Does not treat cross-surface cleanup as todo tidying
Keeps todo scope on the live actionable graph

evals.prompt: The dev server task is stuck -- kill it and start it back up.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-run-processes for stopping and starting managed commands; a todo is not a running process and its lock is not process authority.
assertions: Distinguishes a running process from a todo
Routes to solo-run-processes
Does not treat todo status as process state
Does not use todo locks as process authority

evals.prompt: Set the background worker to auto-start whenever the project opens and restart it if it crashes.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-run-processes for auto-start and crash-restart command policy; todos do not run or supervise commands.
assertions: Recognizes command lifecycle policy
Routes to solo-run-processes
Does not model process supervision as a todo
Keeps todos to actionable work items

evals.prompt: Save the handoff message we keep rewriting as a reusable template with placeholders.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-save-prompts for reusable prompt templates with placeholders; a handoff comment on a todo is per-task, not a template.
assertions: Recognizes reusable template intent
Routes to solo-save-prompts
Distinguishes a per-task handoff comment from a template
Does not store template text in todos

evals.prompt: Fan out the ready todos to parallel agents and coordinate them until the feature merges.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-track-todos; route to $solo-orchestrate-agents for spawning and coordinating parallel workers; the todo skill may surface ready work but does not spawn or drive agents.
assertions: Recognizes multi-agent orchestration
Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents
May surface ready work but does not spawn agents
Does not use todo locks as worker control
